description_long: This is a double-ended wrench with a dark, mottled metallic finish, featuring one open-ended jaw and one closed, ring-shaped jaw. The open end is designed for gripping hexagonal nuts or bolts, while the ring end provides a more secure grip for turning fasteners. The handle is flat and elongated, with a subtle diamond or crosshatch pattern visible along its length, likely for improved grip. The surface appears slightly rough and matte, suggesting a utilitarian tool that may have seen some use. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the body. The overall appearance is robust and functional, suitable for mechanical or automotive tasks in urban environments.
description: A dark, double-ended wrench with one open and one ring jaw, featuring a textured, patterned handle and a rugged, matte finish.
description_view_0: Side view showing both the open and ring ends, with the handle's textured pattern visible.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the circular ring jaw and the thickness of the handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the open and ring jaws, similar to view 0 but reversed.
description_view_3: End-on view focusing on the open jaw, showing the width and cut of the opening.
attribute_handle_grip_pattern: diamond or crosshatch pattern along the handle
attribute_jaw_type: fixed open jaw and fixed ring jaw
